description: O*NET Work Activities (4.A.*) behavioral anchors - Reference for all agents

4.A.1.a.1.name: Getting Information
4.A.1.a.1.description: Observing, receiving, and otherwise obtaining information from all relevant sources.

anchors.description: Follow a standard blueprint
Review a budget
Study international tax laws

4.A.1.a.2.name: Monitoring Processes, Materials, or Surroundings
4.A.1.a.2.description: Monitoring and reviewing information from materials, events, or the environment, to detect or assess problems.

anchors.description: Check to see if baking bread is done
Test electrical circuits
Check the status of a patient in critical medical care

4.A.1.b.1.name: Identifying Objects, Actions, and Events
4.A.1.b.1.description: Identifying information by categorizing, estimating, recognizing differences or similarities, and detecting changes in circumstances or events.

anchors.description: Test an automobile transmission
Judge the suitability of food products for an event
Determine the reaction of a virus to a new drug

4.A.1.b.2.name: Inspecting Equipment, Structures, or Materials
4.A.1.b.2.description: Inspecting equipment, structures, or materials to identify the cause of errors or other problems or defects.

anchors.description: Check that doors to building are locked
Inspect equipment in a chemical processing plant
Conduct a thorough inspection of an aircraft engine

4.A.1.b.3.name: Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information
4.A.1.b.3.description: Estimating sizes, distances, and quantities; or determining time, costs, resources, or materials needed to perform a work activity.

anchors.description: Estimate the size of household furniture to be shipped
Estimate the time required to evacuate a city in the event of a major disaster
Estimate the amount of natural resources that lie beneath the world's oceans

4.A.2.a.1.name: Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
4.A.2.a.1.description: Assessing the value, importance, or quality of things or people.

anchors.description: Determine whether to remove a tree that has been damaged
Determine the value of property lost in a fire
Establish the value of a recently discovered ancient artwork

4.A.2.a.2.name: Processing Information
4.A.2.a.2.description: Compiling, coding, categorizing, calculating, tabulating, auditing, or verifying information or data.

anchors.description: Calculate the costs for shipping packages
Calculate the adjustments for insurance claims
Compile data for a complex scientific report

4.A.2.a.3.name: Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
4.A.2.a.3.description: Using relevant information and individual judgment to determine whether events or processes comply with laws, regulations, or standards.

anchors.description: Review forms for completeness
Evaluate a complicated insurance claim for compliance with policy terms
Make a ruling in court on a complicated motion

4.A.2.a.4.name: Analyzing Data or Information
4.A.2.a.4.description: Identifying the underlying principles, reasons, or facts of information by breaking down information or data into separate parts.

anchors.description: Skim a short article to gather the main point
Determine the interest cost to finance a new building
Analyze the cost of medical care services for all hospitals in the country

4.A.2.b.1.name: Making Decisions and Solving Problems
4.A.2.b.1.description: Analyzing information and evaluating results to choose the best solution and solve problems.

anchors.description: Determine the meal selection for a cafeteria
Select the location for a major retail store
Make the final decision about a company's 5-year plan

4.A.2.b.2.name: Thinking Creatively
4.A.2.b.2.description: Developing, designing, or creating new applications, ideas, relationships, systems, or products, including artistic contributions.

anchors.description: Change the spacing on a printed report
Adapt popular music for a high school marching band
Create new computer software

4.A.2.b.3.name: Updating and Using Relevant Knowledge
4.A.2.b.3.description: Keeping up-to-date technically and applying new knowledge to your job.

anchors.description: Keep up with price changes in a small retail store
Keep current on changes in maintenance procedures for repairing sports cars
Learn information related to a complex and rapidly changing technology

4.A.2.b.4.name: Developing Objectives and Strategies
4.A.2.b.4.description: Establishing long-range objectives and specifying the strategies and actions to achieve them.

anchors.description: Plan the holiday schedule for an airline workforce
Develop the plan to complete the merger of two organizations over a 3-year period
Develop a 10-year business plan for an organization

4.A.2.b.5.name: Scheduling Work and Activities
4.A.2.b.5.description: Scheduling events, programs, and activities, as well as the work of others.

anchors.description: Make appointments for patients using a predetermined schedule
Prepare the work schedule for salesclerks in a large retail store
Schedule a complex conference program with multiple, parallel sessions

4.A.2.b.6.name: Organizing, Planning, and Prioritizing Work
4.A.2.b.6.description: Developing specific goals and plans to prioritize, organize, and accomplish your work.

anchors.description: Organize a work schedule that is repetitive and easy to plan
Plan and adjust a personal to-do list according to changing demands
Prioritize and plan multiple tasks several months ahead

4.A.3.a.1.name: Performing General Physical Activities
4.A.3.a.1.description: Performing general physical activities includes doing activities that require considerable use of your arms and legs and moving your whole body, such as climbing, lifting, balancing, walking, stooping, and handling materials.

anchors.description: Walk between workstations in a small office
Paint the outside of a house
Climb up and down poles to install electricity

4.A.3.a.2.name: Handling and Moving Objects
4.A.3.a.2.description: Using hands and arms in handling, installing, positioning, and moving materials, and manipulating things.

anchors.description: Change settings on copy machines
Arrange books in a library
Load boxes on an assembly line

4.A.3.a.3.name: Controlling Machines and Processes
4.A.3.a.3.description: Using either control mechanisms or direct physical activity to operate machines or processes (not including computers or vehicles).

anchors.description: Operate a cash register
Operate a drilling rig
Operate a precision milling machine

4.A.3.a.4.name: Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment
4.A.3.a.4.description: Running, maneuvering, navigating, or driving vehicles or mechanized equipment, such as forklifts, passenger vehicles, aircraft, or watercraft.

anchors.description: Drive a car
Drive an 18-wheel tractor-trailer
Hover a helicopter in strong wind

4.A.3.b.1.name: Working with Computers
4.A.3.b.1.description: Using computers and computer systems (including hardware and software) to program, write software, set up functions, enter data, or process information.

anchors.description: Enter employee information into a database
Write software for keeping track of parts in inventory
Set up a new computer system for a large multinational company

4.A.3.b.2.name: Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment
4.A.3.b.2.description: Providing documentation, detailed instructions, drawings, or specifications to tell others about how devices, parts, equipment, or structures are to be fabricated, constructed, assembled, modified, maintained, or used.

anchors.description: Specify the lighting for a work area
Specify the furniture and equipment for a new school
Draw the layout of a circuit board for a high-performance computer

4.A.3.b.4.name: Repairing and Maintaining Mechanical Equipment
4.A.3.b.4.description: Servicing, repairing, adjusting, and testing machines, devices, moving parts, and equipment that operate primarily on the basis of mechanical (not electronic) principles.

anchors.description: Make simple, external adjustments to a door hinge with ordinary hand tools
Change the oil on a tractor
Overhaul a power plant turbine

4.A.3.b.5.name: Repairing and Maintaining Electronic Equipment
4.A.3.b.5.description: Servicing, repairing, calibrating, regulating, fine-tuning, or testing machines, devices, and equipment that operate primarily on the basis of electrical or electronic (not mechanical) principles.

anchors.description: Use display settings to adjust a television picture
Make repairs by removing and replacing circuit boards
Use complex test equipment to calibrate electronic equipment

4.A.3.b.6.name: Documenting/Recording Information
4.A.3.b.6.description: Entering, transcribing, recording, storing, or maintaining information in written or electronic/magnetic form.

anchors.description: Record the weight of a patient during a routine health exam
Document the results of a crime scene investigation
Maintain information about the use of satellites for industry communications

4.A.4.a.1.name: Interpreting the Meaning of Information for Others
4.A.4.a.1.description: Translating or explaining what information means and how it can be used.

anchors.description: Interpret a blood pressure reading
Interpret how foreign tax laws apply to U.S. exports
Interpret a complex experiment in physics for general audiences

4.A.4.a.2.name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
4.A.4.a.2.description: Providing information to supervisors, co-workers, and subordinates by telephone, in written form, e-mail, or in person.

anchors.description: Write brief messages to others
Report the results of a sales meeting to a supervisor
Create and deliver a presentation on a company's internal policies

4.A.4.a.3.name: Communicating with People Outside the Organization
4.A.4.a.3.description: Communicating with people outside the organization, representing the organization to customers, the public, government, and other external sources. This information can be exchanged in person, in writing, or by telephone or e-mail.

anchors.description: Have little contact with people outside the organization
Present information to potential clients about available services
Prepare or deliver press releases

4.A.4.a.4.name: Establishing and Maintaining Interpersonal Relationships
4.A.4.a.4.description: Developing constructive and cooperative working relationships with others, and maintaining them over time.

anchors.description: Exchange greetings with a coworker
Maintain good working relationships with almost all coworkers and clients
Gain cooperation from a diverse group of executives with competing interests

4.A.4.a.5.name: Assisting and Caring for Others
4.A.4.a.5.description: Providing personal assistance, medical attention, emotional support, or other personal care to others such as coworkers, customers, or patients.

anchors.description: Help a coworker complete an assignment
Help a medical patient find in-home assistance or healthcare
Care for seriously injured persons in an emergency room

4.A.4.a.6.name: Selling or Influencing Others
4.A.4.a.6.description: Convincing others to buy merchandise/goods or to otherwise change their minds or actions.

anchors.description: Convince a coworker to assist with an assignment
Make a sales pitch to convince others to buy a product
Deliver a major sales campaign in a new market

4.A.4.a.7.name: Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
4.A.4.a.7.description: Handling complaints, settling disputes, and resolving grievances and conflicts, or otherwise negotiating with others.

anchors.description: Apologize to a customer who complains about waiting too long
Get two subordinates to agree about vacation schedules
Negotiate a major labor-management contract

4.A.4.a.8.name: Performing for or Working Directly with the Public
4.A.4.a.8.description: Performing for people or dealing directly with the public. This includes serving customers in restaurants and stores, and receiving clients or guests.

anchors.description: Check tickets at a concert
Sell shoes in a crowded shoe store
Perform a monologue on TV

4.A.4.b.1.name: Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
4.A.4.b.1.description: Getting members of a group to work together to accomplish tasks.

anchors.description: Exchange information during a shift change
Organize the cleanup crew after a major sporting event
Act as general contractor for building a large industrial complex

4.A.4.b.2.name: Developing and Building Teams
4.A.4.b.2.description: Encouraging and building mutual trust, respect, and cooperation among team members.

anchors.description: Encourage two coworkers to stick with a tough assignment
Lead an assembly team in an automobile plant
Lead a large team to design and build a new aircraft

4.A.4.b.3.name: Training and Teaching Others
4.A.4.b.3.description: Identifying the educational needs of others, developing formal educational or training programs or classes, and teaching or instructing others.

anchors.description: Give coworkers brief instructions on a simple procedural change
Teach a social sciences course to high school students
Develop and conduct training programs for a medical school

4.A.4.b.4.name: Guiding, Directing, and Motivating Subordinates
4.A.4.b.4.description: Providing guidance and direction to subordinates, including setting performance standards and monitoring performance.

anchors.description: Work occasionally as a backup supervisor
Manage a small team of employees in a dynamic workplace
Manage a severely downsized unit

4.A.4.b.5.name: Coaching and Developing Others
4.A.4.b.5.description: Identifying the developmental needs of others and coaching, mentoring, or otherwise helping others to improve their knowledge or skills.

anchors.description: Show a coworker how to operate a piece of equipment
Provide on-the-job training for administrative workers
Coach a college athletic team

4.A.4.b.6.name: Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
4.A.4.b.6.description: Providing guidance and expert advice to management or other groups on technical, systems-, or process-related topics.

anchors.description: Work in a position that requires little advising of others
Recommend a new software package to increase operational efficiency
Provide ideas for changing an organization to increase profitability

4.A.4.c.1.name: Performing Administrative Activities
4.A.4.c.1.description: Performing day-to-day administrative tasks such as maintaining information files and processing paperwork.

anchors.description: Complete routine paperwork
Complete tax forms for a small business
Serve as the benefits director for a large computer sales organization

4.A.4.c.2.name: Staffing Organizational Units
4.A.4.c.2.description: Recruiting, interviewing, selecting, hiring, and promoting employees in an organization.

anchors.description: Work in a position that has minimal staffing requirements
Interview candidates for a sales position and make hiring recommendations
Direct a recruiting program for a large international organization

4.A.4.c.3.name: Monitoring and Controlling Resources
4.A.4.c.3.description: Monitoring and controlling resources and overseeing the spending of money.

anchors.description: Work as a server responsible for keeping track of utensils
Work as a chef responsible for ordering food for the menu
Serve as a financial executive in charge of a large company's budget
